articles.title: ‘Nobel Prize’ of mathematics awarded to U of T professor Jacob Tsimerman
articles.summary: Jacob Tsimerman, a professor of mathematics at the University of Toronto, has won the Fields Medal, a high-profile international award often described as the “Nobel Prize for mathematics.” The prestigious award is handed out every four years to mathematicians under the age of 40 and recognizes extraordinary research achievement and the promise of future discoveries. “It […]

articles.title: Suspects posing as landscapers are behind string of break-ins in Durham Region: police
articles.summary: Durham Regional Police are warning residents after a string of break-ins they say were committed by suspects posing as landscapers.

articles.title: Thousands of jobs, billions of dollars on the line in Brampton defence company’s bid to expand
articles.summary: Brampton-based armoured vehicle manufacturer Roshel has already outgrown its 400,000 square feet of manufacturing, research and development space. The decade-old company has become the go-to provider of Light Utility Vehicles (LUVs) to Ukraine and several NATO countries, including the U.S., Germany, the UK and France and is in serious contention to land a multi-billion-dollar deal […]

articles.title: Liquor licence revoked for Etobicoke’s Southside Johnny’s after tribunal finds bar bought stolen liquor for months
articles.summary: According to evidence presented at the hearing, Southside Johnny's owner John Theodorakakis and manager Yasmin Gomes admitted to purchasing stolen goods.

articles.title: Toronto police charge Mississauga man in GTA human trafficking case
articles.summary: Toronto police say a 36‑year‑old Mississauga man has been arrested and charged in a human trafficking investigation that began last month and involved allegations of coercion, deception and financial control over a 32‑year‑old woman. Investigators with the Human Trafficking Unit launched the probe in June 2026, focusing on incidents that occurred in both Toronto and […]

articles.title: Weekend need-to-know: Ossfest among events in Toronto; Gardiner/DVP closures, GO service adjustments
articles.summary: A busy summer weekend ahead as Ossfest, the Toronto Chinatown Festival and the Queen East Streetfest take over Toronto streets. Keep in mind, there will be some major road closures Sunday for the triathlon taking place at the waterfront, along with some GO Transit service adjustments due to construction. OssFest The party on Ossington is […]

articles.title: Newly retired Brampton couple wins $70M Lotto Max jackpot
articles.summary: A newly retired Brampton couple says retirement will be "next level" now they have won a life-changing $70-million Lotto Max jackpot.

articles.title: Fire at Scarborough commercial plaza being investigated as suspicious, authorities say
articles.summary: What started as a vehicle fire spread to a commercial plaza near Eglinton Avenue E. and Kennedy Road early Wednesday morning, Toronto Fire says.
